definition: The process of testing, adjusting and preparing a machine, system or production line for normal operation.

sentences.text: I supported the _____ of the new production line by testing the PLC program.
The _____ process was delayed because one robot failed the safety check.
During _____, we tested every sensor and actuator.

definition: The process of adjusting and checking a measuring device or system to ensure accurate results.

sentences.text: The vision system requires _____ before the camera can measure accurately.
We performed a new TCP _____ after replacing the welding torch.
Incorrect _____ caused a difference between the actual and measured positions.

definition: Not staying the same in quality, performance or behavior over time.

sentences.text: The sensor produced _____ readings during the production test.
_____ weld quality may indicate unstable process parameters.
The robot movement became _____ because the fixture was not secured.

definition: A signal that appears and disappears irregularly instead of remaining stable.

sentences.text: The proximity sensor generated an _____ because the cable was damaged.
We monitored the PLC input to identify the source of the _____.
An _____ caused the machine to stop several times during the shift.

definition: A period when a machine, system or production line is not operating.

sentences.text: Preventive maintenance helped reduce unplanned _____ on the production line.
The damaged sensor caused thirty minutes of _____ during the night shift.
The engineering team analyzed the fault to prevent further _____.

definition: Greater than what is normal, necessary or acceptable.

sentences.text: _____ vibration may damage the motor bearings over time.
The inspection system detected _____ sealant on the part surface.
_____ welding current can cause spatter and poor weld quality.

definition: The fundamental reason why a problem or failure occurred.

sentences.text: The team used the Five Whys method to identify the _____ of the repeated alarm.
Replacing the sensor solved the symptom but not the _____.
We reviewed the fault history to determine the _____ of the production stop.

definition: To disconnect or switch off the electrical energy supplied to equipment.

sentences.text: Always _____ before opening the electrical cabinet.
The technician had to _____ from the drive before replacing the motor cable.
Press the emergency stop and _____ before starting maintenance work.

definition: Not clearly expressed, explained or understood because important details are missing.

sentences.text: The instructions were too _____ for the new employee to follow.
He gave a _____ answer and avoided explaining the real problem.
The project requirements are still _____ and need to be clarified.

definition: To test and prepare a machine, system or production line so that it can begin normal operation.

sentences.text: The engineering team will _____ the new robotic cell next week.
We must test every safety device before we _____ the machine.
The customer asked us to _____ the production line before Friday.

definition: To adjust a measuring device, sensor or system so that it produces accurate results.

sentences.text: We need to _____ the camera before starting the measurement process.
The technician used a reference part to _____ the sensor.
Always _____ the robot TCP after replacing the welding torch.

definition: The amount of material, products or work that a system can process within a specific period of time.

sentences.text: The path optimization increased the cell _____ without adding another robot.
Frequent stops reduced the production line's hourly _____.
We measured _____ to determine whether the new conveyor met the target capacity.

definition: A safety or control feature that prevents equipment from operating unless specific conditions are satisfied.

sentences.text: The safety _____ prevents the robot from moving while the door is open.
A missing _____ signal stopped the automatic cycle.
We tested the door _____ before releasing the cell for production.

definition: The use of additional components or systems so that operation or safety can continue if one component fails.

sentences.text: The safety circuit uses _____ to reduce the risk of a single-point failure.
Sensor _____ allows the controller to compare two independent signals.
The design includes power-supply _____ for critical equipment.

definition: To make two or more machines, processes or movements operate at the same time or in the correct sequence.

sentences.text: The PLC must _____ the conveyor with the robot's picking motion.
We used handshake signals to _____ the three robots.
The controller can _____ both servo axes during the transfer operation.

definition: To identify the cause of a fault or problem by examining its symptoms and available information.

sentences.text: The engineer used the alarm history to _____ the communication fault.
It is difficult to _____ an intermittent problem without recorded data.
We checked the PLC inputs to _____ why the cylinder was not moving.

definition: To confirm through testing or evidence that a system, process or result meets its requirements.

sentences.text: We performed a dry run to _____ the robot program before production.
The quality team will _____ the inspection results using reference samples.
Every safety function must be tested to _____ the final system.

definition: To improve a process, system or design so that it performs as effectively or efficiently as possible.

sentences.text: We changed the robot path to _____ the cycle time.
The engineer adjusted the welding parameters to _____ process quality.
Production data helped us _____ the material flow between stations.
